title: 10 best herbal teas for jaw clenching
intro_desc: Herbal teas for jaw clenching are natural remedies made from various plants and herbs that help alleviate the symptoms of teeth grinding and jaw clenching, also known as bruxism. These teas often contain soothing ingredients like chamomile, valerian root, and lemon balm, which promote relaxation and reduce muscle tension. By drinking these teas before bedtime, individuals can prevent jaw clenching and teeth grinding during sleep, leading to improved sleep quality and reduced jaw pain. Furthermore, regular consumption of herbal teas can also help manage stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for bruxism. As a result, many people have reported significant improvements in their overall well-being and quality of life after incorporating herbal teas into their daily routine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tea helps with jaw clenching because it contains certain compounds, such as bisabolol and chamazulene, that have anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties. These compounds can help reduce inflammation and tension in the jaw muscles, thereby alleviating the discomfort and pain associated with jaw clenching. Additionally, chamomile tea is known for its calming and sedative effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety, two common triggers of jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, German chamomile tea can provide relief from jaw clenching and improve overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the jaw muscles, alleviating tension and discomfort caused by clenching. This is mainly due to the presence of active constituents like chamazulene and bisabolol.

Muscle relaxant: German chamomile tea contains muscle relaxant properties that can help alleviate muscle spasms and tension in the jaw, promoting relaxation and reducing clenching. The primary compound responsible for this effect is matricin, which is converted to the anti-inflammatory compound chamazulene during steam distillation.

Anxiolytic: German chamomile tea has anxiolytic properties that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help reduce jaw clenching caused by stress or anxiety. The active constituents responsible for this effect include apigenin and luteolin, which bind to benzodiazepine receptors in the brain.

Analgesic: German chamomile tea has analgesic properties that can help alleviate pain and discomfort in the jaw caused by clenching. The main active constituents responsible for this effect include several flavonoids, terpenoids, and essential oils.

Antioxidant: German chamomile tea has antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to jaw clenching. The active constituents responsible for this effect include several polyphenols, flavonoids, and terpenoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, chamomile flowers have anti-inflammatory and muscle relaxant properties, making them ideal for soothing jaw clenching.
Leaves: Containing essential oils and antioxidants, chamomile leaves help reduce inflammation and provide a calming effect, alleviating tension in the jaw.
Seeds: High in essential oils and fatty acids, chamomile seeds can help relax muscles and reduce inflammation, providing relief for jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried German chamomile flowers.
Place the chamomile flowers in a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour 8 ounces of boiling water over the chamomile flowers.
Allow the tea to steep for 5-10 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea, then enjoy.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tea helps with jaw clenching because it contains compounds that have muscle-relaxing properties. One of these compounds is rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to help reduce muscle tension and spasms. Additionally, sage tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing. This can be especially beneficial for people who clench their jaws due to stress or anxiety, as these conditions can cause inflammation and muscle tension. By drinking sage tea, individuals may be able to relieve muscle tension in the jaw, reducing the frequency and severity of jaw clenching. Furthermore, the soothing and calming effects of sage tea may help reduce stress and anxiety, which can also contribute to jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Sage tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and pain in the jaw muscles, providing relief from clenching. This is due to the presence of compounds such as rosmarinic acid and salvianolic acid.
Analgesic: Sage tea can help alleviate jaw pain caused by clenching, thanks to its analgesic properties. This is attributed to the presence of essential oils like camphor, eucalyptol, and borneol.

Muscle relaxant: Sage tea has muscle relaxant properties that can help reduce tension in the jaw muscles, thereby preventing clenching. This is due to the presence of active constituents like rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Antioxidant: Sage tea's high antioxidant content helps protect the jaw muscles from oxidative stress and damage, promoting muscle health and reducing the likelihood of clenching. This is due to the presence of phenolic acids, flavonoids, and other antioxidant compounds.

Adaptogenic: Sage tea has adaptogenic properties that can help the body cope with stress, which can contribute to jaw clenching. This is due to the presence of active constituents like rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and various essential oils.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in soothing compounds, sage leaves help relax muscles and alleviate jaw clenching.
Buds: Packed with anti-inflammatory properties, sage buds can help reduce tension and inflammation in the jaw.
Stems: Containing calming agents, sage stems can help promote relaxation and reduce jaw clenching.
Bark: With muscle-relaxing properties, sage bark is an effective addition to medicinal tea for jaw clenching relief.
Roots: Known for their stress-reducing abilities, sage roots are a valuable component in calming jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water in a kettle or pot for 5-10 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried sage leaves to a tea infuser or teapot for 3-5 minutes.
Pour the hot water over the sage leaves and let them steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea to discard the sage leaves.
Drink the sage tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with jaw clenching because it contains natural compounds that have a calming effect on the body. The tea is known for its ability to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate the tension that often leads to jaw clenching. Additionally, lemon balm has anti-spasmodic properties, which can help to relax the muscles in the jaw and face, providing relief from clenching and tension. The soothing warmth of the tea can also help to increase blood flow to the area, further aiding in relaxation and reducing inflammation. Overall, lemon balm tea can be a natural and effective way to manage jaw clenching and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Lemon balm tea helps reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which is important for jaw clenching as tension and anxiety can often trigger or worsen the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and citral.

Sedative: Lemon balm tea has mild sedative properties that can help alleviate tension and promote better sleep, which is crucial for those who clench their jaws due to stress or insomnia. The primary compound responsible for this effect is rosmarinic acid.

Antispasmodic: Lemon balm tea can help relieve muscle spasms and tension, making it useful for reducing jaw clenching and associated pain. The active constituents responsible for this property are eugenol and tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the jaw area, providing relief from clenching-induced pain. The main compounds responsible for this effect are rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.

Nervine: Lemon balm tea acts as a nervine, helping to soothe and support the nervous system. This is important for jaw clenching as it can help alleviate symptoms caused by nervous tension and help restore balance to the nervous system. The active constituents responsible for this property are rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of lemon balm tea plant are commonly used in making medicinal tea for jaw clenching due to their soothing and calming properties.
Flowers: The flowers of lemon balm tea plant are rich in antioxidants and have a calming effect, making them a good choice for medicinal tea for jaw clenching.
Stems: The stems of lemon balm tea plant contain therapeutic compounds that can help alleviate tension and reduce jaw clenching.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon balm tea plant are a great source of essential oils and acids, which can help relax the muscles and alleviate jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh lemon balm leaves or use 1 tablespoon of dried leaves.
Rinse the lemon balm leaves under cold water to remove any dirt or impurities.
Place the lemon balm leaves in a tea infuser or teapot and add 8 ounces of boiling water.
Let the tea steep for 5-10 minutes to allow the herb to release its oils and active compounds.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea, then drink it while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tea helps with jaw clenching because it contains passionflower, an ingredient that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. Jaw clenching can often be a result of tension and stress, and passionflower may help alleviate this by acting as a natural sedative. It is thought to increase the levels of a chemical called gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain, which can have a calming effect on the nervous system. By promoting relaxation and reducing tension, maypop tea may help to ease jaw clenching and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Maypop tea contains alkaloids such as chrysin and skullcapflavone II, which help to relax muscles, reducing tension and jaw clenching.
Anti-anxiety: The flavonoids present in maypop tea, like oroxylin A, have anxiolytic effects, which can help alleviate anxiety-induced jaw clenching.
Analgesic: Maypop tea contains various alkaloids, such as passionflorine, which have pain-relieving properties, helping to reduce discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids in maypop tea, such as luteolin, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help decrease inflammation and swelling in the jaw area.
Sedative: The alkaloids and flavonoids in maypop tea, like 5,7-dimethoxyflavone, can act as sedatives, promoting relaxation and reducing the likelihood of jaw clenching during sleep.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maypop tea plant contain high levels of antioxidants and alkaloids, which can help relax muscles and reduce tension in the jaw.
Leaves: The leaves of the maypop tea plant have been traditionally used to treat nervous disorders, including jaw clenching, due to their calming and relaxing effects.
Flowers: The flowers of the maypop tea plant contain active compounds that can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate jaw clenching.
Seeds: The seeds of the maypop tea plant are rich in essential fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help reduce inflammation and promote muscle relaxation in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried maypop (Passiflora incarnata) leaves and place them in a tea infuser or teapot.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 2-3 minutes before pouring it over the maypop leaves.
Steep the tea for 5-10 minutes, depending on desired strength.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed for jaw clenching relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap tea helps with jaw clenching because it contains muscle relaxant properties that can help alleviate tension in the jaw and surrounding muscles. The herb works by reducing inflammation and spasms in the muscles, which can help to relieve the discomfort and pain associated with jaw clenching. Additionally, skullcap tea is known to have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety, two common triggers of jaw clenching. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, skullcap tea can provide relief for those who experience jaw clenching, allowing them to enjoy improved jaw function and reduced discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic: Skullcap tea can help reduce anxiety, which is important for jaw clenching as anxiety can often trigger or worsen the habit. The active constituents believed to contribute to this property include flavonoids, such as baicalin and scutellarin.

Muscle relaxant: Skullcap tea has been found to have muscle relaxant properties, which can help alleviate tension in the jaw muscles and reduce clenching. This is thought to be due to the presence of constituents like scutellarin and catalpol.

Antispasmodic: Skullcap tea may help relieve muscle spasms, including those in the jaw, by acting as a natural antispasmodic. This property can be attributed to the herb's flavonoid and iridoid content.

Sedative: Skullcap tea has a sedative effect, which can promote relaxation and help reduce jaw clenching, particularly during stressful situations or at night. The sedative properties are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, volatile oils, and bitter iridoid glycosides.

Anti-inflammatory: Skullcap tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the jaw muscles and alleviate pain associated with clenching. This property is attributed to the herb's phenolic acid, flavonoid, and iridoid content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain high levels of antioxidants and relaxing compounds, making them effective in reducing muscle tension and jaw clenching.
Buds: Rich in essential oils and bioactive compounds, buds can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are often linked to jaw clenching.
Flowers: Packed with anti-inflammatory properties, flowers can help soothe tight jaw muscles and reduce pain.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids and nutrients, promoting overall muscle relaxation and easing jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 2 teaspoons of dried skullcap herb.
Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool for 1 minute.
Steep the skullcap herb in the hot water for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the herb from the tea and discard it.
Sip the skullcap tea as needed for jaw clenching, up to three times a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with jaw clenching because it contains certain compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, that have relaxing and calming effects on the body. These compounds can help to reduce muscle tension and promote a sense of relaxation, which can in turn help to alleviate symptoms of jaw clenching. Additionally, lavender has been shown to have anti-anxiety and stress-relieving properties, which may help to address some of the underlying causes of jaw clenching. By reducing muscle tension, promoting relaxation, and alleviating anxiety, English lavender tea can be a natural and effective way to help manage symptoms of jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Anxiolytic property: English lavender tea can help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which is important for jaw clenching as tension and stress often trigger this condition. The active constituents responsible for this property are linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been shown to have anxiolytic effects.

Analgesic property: English lavender tea has pain-relieving properties that can alleviate the discomfort associated with jaw clenching. This is achieved through the presence of compounds such as cineole and borneol, which have been found to have analgesic effects.

Anti-inflammatory property: English lavender tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the jaw muscles, thereby alleviating tension and pain. The active constituents responsible for this property include rosmarinic acid and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory effects.

Spasmolytic property: English lavender tea can help relax muscle spasms, including those in the jaw, thereby reducing clenching and tension. This is achieved through the presence of compounds such as linalool and linalyl acetate, which have been found to have spasmolytic effects.

Sedative property: English lavender tea has a sedative effect that can promote better sleep, which is important for jaw clenching as poor sleep or insomnia can exacerbate the condition. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool and lavandulol, which have been found to have sedative effects.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids, the flowers of English lavender tea plant have calming properties that help alleviate jaw clenching.
Leaves: Containing volatile oils, the leaves of the English lavender tea plant offer soothing effects, reducing tension in the jaw.
Buds: High in antioxidants, the buds of the English lavender tea plant provide potent anti-inflammatory benefits, helping to relax the jaw muscles.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water and let it cool slightly, so it reaches a temperature of 205-212°F (96-100°C).
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried English lavender flowers to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the lavender flowers and let them steep for 5-7 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the lavender flowers from the teapot.
Drink the herbal lavender tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed for jaw clenching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with jaw clenching because it contains natural compounds that have muscle-relaxing properties. The tea is made from the leaves of a tropical tree called Litsea glutinosa, which is native to Southeast Asia. The leaves are rich in a compound called limonene, which has been shown to have relaxant effects on smooth muscles. This can help to alleviate tension in the jaw muscles, reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching. Additionally, the tea contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the jaw joints and muscles. Drinking large-leaved lime tea regularly may help to relieve symptoms of temporomandibular joint disorder (TMJ) and other conditions that cause jaw pain and tension.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Large-leaved lime tea contains antispasmodic compounds, such as limonene and flavonoids, which help relax muscle tension and alleviate jaw clenching.
Anti-inflammatory: The tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to its rich polyphenol content, reduce inflammation in the jaw area, easing discomfort from clenching.
Anxiolytic: Large-leaved lime tea has calming effects, containing constituents like kaempferol that help reduce anxiety and stress-induced jaw clenching.
Analgesic: The tea's pain-relieving properties, attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids, help alleviate pain caused by persistent jaw clenching.
Neuroprotective: Large-leaved lime tea can help protect neurons, as it contains compounds like luteolin that may reduce damage from repetitive jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in tannins, the leaves help to relax muscles and alleviate jaw clenching.
Buds: High in antioxidants, buds can help reduce inflammation and provide soothing effects on the jaw.
Bark: Contains therapeutic compounds, the bark can help ease muscle spasms and provide relief for tension in the jaw.
remedy_recipe: Boil 4 cups of water and let it cool down to 212 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a teapot, add 2 tablespoons of dried large-leaved lime leaves and 1 tablespoon of dried chamomile flowers.
Pour the hot water over the herbs and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey and 1/2 teaspoon of dried crushed cloves for flavor (optional).
Strain the tea into a cup and enjoy it while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed for jaw clenching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Common lime tea helps with jaw clenching because it contains natural compounds that have muscle relaxant properties. The tea is made from the leaves of the lime tree, which contain antioxidants and other compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and muscle tension. Drinking common lime tea can help to relax the muscles in the jaw, reducing the frequency and intensity of jaw clenching. Additionally, the warm temperature of the tea can help to soothe sore muscles and promote relaxation, further reducing jaw tension. By helping to relax the muscles and reduce inflammation, common lime tea can provide relief from jaw clenching and help to prevent related symptoms such as headaches and ear pain.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Lime tea contains compounds such as flavonoids and terpenes which have natural muscle relaxant properties, helping to alleviate tension in the jaw muscles caused by clenching.
Anti-inflammatory: The antioxidants and vitamin C present in lime tea exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, reducing inflammation in the jaw area and providing relief from clenching.
Analgesic: Lime tea's analgesic properties, attributed to its limonene and citral content, help manage pain and discomfort associated with jaw clenching.
Sedative: Certain compounds found in lime tea, including flavonoids and volatile oils, possess sedative qualities that can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are often linked to jaw clenching.
Antispasmodic: Lime tea's antispasmodic properties, due to its magnesium and potassium content, aid in preventing muscle spasms and reducing the frequency of jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants, the leaves help reduce inflammation and muscle tension, providing relief for jaw clenching.
Flowers: Containing calming properties, the flowers aid in stress reduction, helping to alleviate symptoms of jaw clenching.
Buds: High in soothing agents, the buds help relax muscles, providing a natural remedy for jaw clenching.
Stems: Packed with therapeutic compounds, the stems contribute to reducing muscle spasms and inflammation in the jaw.
Roots: Bursting with medicinal properties, the roots help alleviate pain and promote relaxation, reducing instances of jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Boil 2 cups of water and add 2 teaspoons of dried lime flower leaves, letting it steep for 10 minutes.
Crush 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers and add to the lime flower tea, steeping both for an additional 5 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the herbs.
Add honey to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed for jaw clenching.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian tea helps with jaw clenching because it contains natural compounds that have muscle relaxant properties. These compounds, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work by reducing the activity of nerve cells in the brain, which can help to alleviate muscle tension and spasms. By promoting relaxation and reducing muscle tension, valerian tea can help to relieve the symptoms of jaw clenching, which is often caused by stress, anxiety, or teeth grinding. Additionally, valerian tea is caffeine-free, making it a gentle and soothing option for those looking for a natural way to manage jaw clenching.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Valerian tea contains active constituents like valepotriates and alkaloids that help relax muscles, reducing tension and jaw clenching.
Anxiolytic: Its calming effects, due to the presence of valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, can alleviate anxiety-induced jaw clenching by promoting relaxation and mental well-being.
Sedative: Valerian tea can help reduce jaw clenching by inducing sleep and improving sleep quality, as it increases the amount of gamma-aminobutyric acid (GABA) in the brain.
Anti-inflammatory: The presence of antioxidants in valerian tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can help reduce inflammation and pain associated with excessive jaw clenching.
Antispasmodic: Valerian tea's antispasmodic properties, resulting from its active components like valtrate and isovaltrate, can help alleviate muscle spasms and involuntary jaw clenching.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian tea made from the roots is commonly used to treat jaw clenching due to its calming and relaxing properties.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the valerian plant are used in teas to alleviate muscle tension, including jaw clenching, because of their muscle relaxant properties.
Flowers: Valerian flowers are used in teas to help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help alleviate jaw clenching.
Leaves: Valerian leaves can be used in teas to help reduce muscle spasms, which can help relieve jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, add 1 teaspoon of dried valerian root to 1 cup of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the herb's beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the solid plant material, leaving only the liquid.
Optional: Add honey or lemon to taste, as desired for flavor.
Drink the tea while it is still warm, up to three times a day as needed for jaw clenching relief.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Humulus lupulus tea, also known as hops tea, helps with jaw clenching because it contains natural compounds that have muscle relaxant properties. These compounds, including dimethylvinyl carbinol and humulene, can help to reduce muscle tension and spasms, which can be a contributing factor to jaw clenching. Additionally, hops have been shown to have calming and sedative effects, which can help to reduce anxiety and stress, further helping to alleviate jaw clenching. The warm temperature of the tea can also help to soothe and relax the muscles in the jaw, providing additional relief. Drinking hops tea can be a natural and effective way to help manage jaw clenching and find relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Muscle relaxant: Humulus lupulus tea contains compounds called humulones and lupulones, which have muscle relaxant properties, helping to alleviate jaw clenching by reducing tension in the muscles.

Anxiolytic: The tea has been found to have anxiolytic effects, which can help reduce anxiety and stress, often associated with jaw clenching; the active constituents responsible for this property are the essential oils and flavonoids present in the plant.

Anti-inflammatory: Humulus lupulus tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the jaw muscles, thus alleviating pain and discomfort caused by clenching; this is attributed to the presence of β-acids and specific flavonoids.

Sedative: The tea has sedative effects, which can help promote relaxation and reduce teeth grinding or jaw clenching, particularly during sleep; this property is mainly due to the presence of essential oils, specifically myrcene and humulene.

Analgesic: Humulus lupulus tea has analgesic properties, which can help alleviate pain caused by jaw clenching; this is attributed to the presence of various compounds, including cannabinoids and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers of the humulus lupulus plant, also known as hops, contain relaxing properties that can help alleviate muscle tension and reduce jaw clenching.
Leaves: The leaves of the plant have similar relaxing properties to the flowers, and can be used as an alternative when flowers are not available.
Stems: The stems of the plant contain compounds that can help reduce inflammation and muscle tension, making them useful for making medicinal tea for jaw clenching.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the plant, also known as the underground stems, contain high levels of volatile oils that have a calming effect on the nervous system, and can help reduce jaw clenching.

Buds: The buds of the plant, also known as the unopened flowers, contain high levels of essential oils that have a sedative effect on the body, making them useful for making medicinal tea for jaw clenching.
remedy_recipe: Boil 8 ounces of water in a teapot or saucepan for 3-5 minutes.
Add 1-2 teaspoons of dried hops (Humulus lupulus) flowers to a tea infuser or teapot.
Pour the hot water over the hops and let it steep for 5-10 minutes.
Remove the tea infuser or strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea while it's still warm, up to three times a day as needed for jaw clenching.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat jaw clenching with herbal teas is by regularly drinking chamomile or lemon balm infusions. These teas have calming properties that can help reduce tension in the jaw and promote relaxation. To prepare, pour boiling water over a tea bag or loose leaves and let it steep for several minutes. Drink two to three cups daily, especially before bedtime, to alleviate symptoms. However, if jaw clenching persists or is accompanied by severe pain, consult a healthcare professional for proper evaluation and treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to jaw clenching that are treated with herbal teas include tension headaches and anxiety. Chamomile tea is known for its calming properties and can help reduce tension in the jaw and related headaches. Similarly, lemon balm tea can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, which can help prevent jaw clenching. Passionflower tea is another option that can help reduce anxiety and promote sleep, which can also help alleviate jaw clenching. Peppermint tea can help relax muscles and reduce pain, making it a good choice for treating tension headaches related to jaw clenching. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using herbal teas as a treatment for any ailment.
